title: Slow Sous Korean Pork Ssam
description: A traditional Korean dish featuring tender pork wrapped in lettuce leaves, this recipe incorporates sesame seeds and sous-vide cooking for a nostalgic and earthy flavor profile.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide machine to 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, combine sesame oil, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Place the pork shoulder in a large sous-vide bag and pour the marinade over the pork.
Seal the bag and cook the pork in the sous-vide machine for 24 hours.
After 24 hours, remove the pork from the bag and slice it into thin strips.
In a small bowl, mix together sesame seeds, salt, and black pepper.
Sprinkle the sesame seed mixture over the sliced pork.
Assemble the ssam by placing a few slices of the pork onto a lettuce leaf and topping with green onions.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
